etymology.description: semantic ⺮(竹) (zhú, "bamboo") + phonetic 即(即) (jí) -> node of bamboo -> segment or boundary
etymology.interpretation: The 'node' sense originates from the appearance of nodes on a bamboo stalk, where sections divide. This visible segmentation extends metaphorically to signify any point of division or boundary, such as a segment of time or space.
etymology.simplification: The traditional 節 was simplified to 节 by replacing the bamboo radical ⺮(竹) with 艹(艹), a simpler component, while retaining the phonetic 即(即).

examples.chinese: 树枝上有很多节(樹枝上有很多節)。
examples.pinyin: Shùzhī shàng yǒu hěn duō jié.
examples.english: There are many nodes on the branch.

examples.chinese: 每个节都很重要(每個節都很重要)。
examples.pinyin: Měi gè jié dōu hěn zhòngyào.

examples.chinese: 节的功能影响了植物的生长(節的功能影響了植物的生長)。
examples.pinyin: Jié de gōngnéng yǐngxiǎngle zhíwù de shēngzhǎng.
examples.english: The function of nodes affects the growth of the plant.
